Items.Key: command injection in admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|608|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|system(cmd.c_str());|remote code execution on the host system via direct execution of unvalidated user-supplied commands.|authenticated admin user executing commands prefixed with "exec".|remove system() usage. implement a strict command whitelist or use safe execution apis with argument arrays.->command injection in admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|608|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|system(cmd.c_str());|remote code execution on the host system via direct execution of unvalidated user-supplied commands.|authenticated admin user executing commands prefixed with "exec".|remove system() usage. implement a strict command whitelist or use safe execution apis with argument arrays.

out-of-bounds write in safe string copy|buffer overflow|cwe-787|medium|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|203|205|string_utils::safe_string_copy|if (src_len > dest_size) src_len = dest_size; memcpy(dest, src, src_len); dest[src_len] = '\0';|heap/stack buffer overflow when src_len equals dest_size, writing the null terminator past the allocated buffer boundary.|source string length matches or exceeds the destination buffer size.|cap copy length to dest_size - 1 to guarantee space for the null terminator.->out-of-bounds write in safe string copy|buffer overflow|cwe-787|medium|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|203|205|string_utils::safe_string_copy|if (src_len > dest_size) src_len = dest_size; memcpy(dest, src, src_len); dest[src_len] = '\0';|heap/stack buffer overflow when src_len equals dest_size, writing the null terminator past the allocated buffer boundary.|source string length matches or exceeds the destination buffer size.|cap copy length to dest_size - 1 to guarantee space for the null terminator.

truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 correctly identified 8 of the 20 true vulnerabilities, missing 12. It also reported 2 findings not present in the ground truth. Severity and CWE classifications were partially accurate but contained notable mismatches for format string and heap overflow vulnerabilities.

Items.Notes: Run 2 explicitly identified the format string vulnerability in the logging module with matching evidence.
Integer overflow in the factorial computation loop was not mentioned in Run 2.
Use-after-free in memory pool cleanup during deferred history flush was not mentioned.
Run 2 finding 10 directly matches the command injection via config validation with identical evidence.
Run 2 finding 4 correctly identified the stack buffer overflow in input processing.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "evidence": "long long old_value = value_; std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_); value_ = old_value + 1;"
Items.Notes: Run 2 finding 7 accurately captured the race condition in the thread-safe counter.
Logic bomb via EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE bypass was not identified.
Run 2 finding 9 correctly flagged path traversal in config loading.
Run 2 identified the heap buffer overflow in the parser but misclassified it as CWE-120/High instead of CWE-122/Critical.
Run 2 finding 6 correctly identified hardcoded administrative credentials.
Weak PRNG for session tokens was not mentioned.
Thread-unsafe timestamp cache in logging was not identified.
Insecure temporary file handling was not mentioned.
Persistent authentication state was not flagged.
Unhandled exception in variable parsing was not identified.
Integer underflow in memory-pool block split was not mentioned.
Unbounded recursion in parser was not identified.
Data race in result cache was not mentioned.
TOCTOU in config loading was not identified.
Run 2 finding 8 correctly identified integer division by zero in route score calculation.
